rationale: The citing paper uses Kumar and Pansari (2016) in core theory development, not just as a background list citation. It is cited to support the claim that consumer engagement drives purchasing behavior, revenue, and firm value, and this logic directly motivates the paper's downstream hypothesis linking rivalry references to purchase intentions via engagement. The citation therefore plays a meaningful conceptual role in framing why engagement outcomes matter, even though the paper does not reuse the cited paper's specific measurement framework.
relation_description: The citing paper treats the cited paper as prior foundational evidence that customer engagement has consequential effects on purchases and firm performance. This foundation is used to justify the importance of engagement in the model and to motivate the serial mediation prediction toward purchase intentions.
